name: Anthurium forgetii × crystallinum
origin: Horticultural hybrid; derived from tropical American parentage
aboutText: Anthurium forgetii × crystallinum is a cultivated horticultural hybrid combining two of the most celebrated velvet anthuriums in the hobby. From the forgetii parent it inherits the distinctive reduced to nearly closed sinus — that rounded, almost seamless base where the two leaf lobes meet — and from crystallinum it draws the striking silvery-white primary venation that fans across the velvety deep green blade with impressive clarity. The result is a compact, self-heading plant with broad, rounded cordate leaves that combine the best visual qualities of both parents in a single, manageable specimen. Among the intermediate velvet anthurium hybrids, forgetii × crystallinum is widely regarded as one of the most elegant and visually satisfying crosses, offering the impact of both parents without the demanding care requirements of the most extreme species.

morphology.leafShape: Broadly rounded cordate, sinus reduced to nearly closed
morphology.venation: Striking silvery-white primary venation on deep velvety green blade
